items.blurb: Hello Kitty and friends line up to hold your reminders — six pads of pure planner joy for under seven dollars.
Your poor charger has been naked this whole time. Fix that with a squishy 3D Hello Kitty suit and a glossy pink cable wrap.
Twenty translucent sakura push pins that make even your tax reminders look like spring in Kyoto.
Nine rolls of bright geometrics with gold-foil sparkle — the washi stash upgrade your journal has been hinting about.
Planets, constellations and foil-flecked moons on midnight rolls — tape a whole night sky across your spreads.
Eight rolls of florals and geometry in every blue you can name, shot through with a whisper of silver foil.
Give your charger a puffy 3D heart makeover — squishy silicone armour that flirts every time you plug in.
Chu Totoro in painterly form on a sturdy tote — errands feel gentler with a forest spirit carrying your library haul.
Die-cut papers that mimic grandma's finest lacework — layer them under photos and suddenly your journal has heirlooms.
A palm-sized sketchbook wrapped in wildflowers, ready to catch every doodle before it escapes on the bus.
Slide the little meter and let your jacket do the talking — an enamel pin that announces your energy level so you don't have to.
Astronaut Snoopy guards your transit card in a slim clear-backed case — six designs, all of them beagle excellence.
A five-dollar retractable with Peanuts comic strips wrapped around the barrel — the cheapest serotonin in your pencil case.
Kittens tumbling through golden leaves across four cover designs — autumn journaling with maximum whisker content.
Embroidered Miffy on pastel gingham, sized for lip balm, cards and the small chaos at the bottom of your bag.
Sakura blooms up the barrel, a cat on top, and heart confetti floating in glitter — grocery lists never stood a chance.
A pen-shaped craft blade for clean journal cuts — handy, but more toolbox than treasure.
Hello Kitty etched in glowing acrylic on a wooden base, cycling through colours — part lamp, part tiny shrine.
Chunky silicone Snoopy and pals dangling from your keys — pocket-sized nostalgia that survives being sat on.
A rotating four-compartment caddy staffed by Sanrio characters — desk chaos, meet your adorable new manager.
The 2025 Mildliner drop: five smokey, calm tones that highlight without shouting — study aesthetic secured.
Press the character's ears to swap black ink for red — Sanrio engineering at its most delightfully unnecessary.
Four pens, each dressed in its character's signature colour — a matching Sanrio squad for your pencil cup.
Illustrated travel scenes that read like postcards from places you haven't been yet — wanderlust in sticker form.
Tombow's beloved Mono Graph mechanical pencil, but make it Sanrio — serious drafting tool, deeply unserious exterior.
Thirty leaf-shaped cards for notes that deserve better than a text — nature's stationery, ready to mail.
A plush embroidered tote where Totoro and Chibi Totoro hoard acorns — soft enough to hug, roomy enough for errands.
A whole roll of realistic autumn leaves ready to drift across your planner — foliage season, no raking required.
Kiki, Jiji and Lily on three official Movic sticky pads — your to-do list just got a delivery-witch upgrade.
A wide washi ribbon of misty peaks and green valleys — unroll a landscape painting straight across the page.
Fifty tiny hearts to pin your vision board together — because inspiration deserves better than beige thumbtacks.
Six mini highlighters shaped like grinning cartoon animals — study sessions, now with a tiny cheering squad.
Soft watercolour washes in blue and pink, cut into neat geometric pads — reminders that look like tiny paintings.
Two helpful little bears hold your to-do list on these Furukawashiko sticky notes — accountability was never this soft.
Twelve creamy Japanese Gansai watercolours with a glossy finish — traditional pigments that make beginners look gifted.
Fifty-six pieces of aged botanical ephemera for collaging — instant time-worn charm without raiding an antique shop.
Chibi Usagi and Chibiusa cast in dreamy enamel — moon-princess energy for your tote strap or pin board.
Sophie and Howl rendered as elegant portrait pins — wearable stills from the moving castle itself.
Sepia coffee cups, old letters and café ephemera in glossy waterproof stickers — a nostalgic espresso for your journal.
A square pocket planner with its own pen — schedule, goals and reflections in one tidy little package.
Twenty-eight textured kraft sheets in botanical moods — the earthy base layer every scrapbook spread wants.
Mt. Fuji rises from your paperwork in deep or light blue clip form — a tiny mountain range for the in-tray.
Reliable eco-friendly pencil lead refills — useful, but strictly a supply run, not a swoon.
A smiling pastel snail whose shell swallows your pens — the slowest, sweetest fix for desk clutter.
A 3D Snoopy lounges on the silicone lid keeping your coffee warm — limited edition, maximum morning comfort.
Thirty ounces of insulated ombré with Snoopy along for the ride — hydration goals, but make them limited edition.
A precision eraser beloved by artists — excellent at its job, just not exactly shelf candy.
A full deck printed with scenes from Totoro or Spirited Away — game night, now directed by Miyazaki.
Extra-wide watercolour washi of mountains, clouds and coastlines — landscapes that unroll by the inch.
Glossy clear stickers of the year turning — sakura mountains to snowy cabins, all in soft watercolour.
Wisteria and grapevines trail across clear tape — borders that look hand-painted, zero artistic risk required.
Cottages, rolling fields and dreamy mountains in soft painterly tape — a countryside escape by the roll.
Twenty rose-gold heart clips in a clear box — the fastest way to make a boring stack of paper feel like a love letter.
A fluffy embroidered pouch where Totoro guards his acorns — forest-floor softness for your everyday essentials.
Bold comic-cover notebooks starring Snoopy, Charlie Brown, Woodstock or Belle — sixty lined pages of retro cool.
Watercolour florals kissed with gold foil on glossy stickers — instant garden-party elegance for any spread.
A potted-flower lamp that glows from the middle of its bloom, petals rotating — part night light, part happy houseplant.
A 2-in-1 charging cable dressed in Sanrio pastels — even your battery percentage deserves a cute delivery.
Clear stackable drawers with Sanrio flair — washi, stickers and clips finally get the cute filing system they earned.
A pastel A5 notebook with a customisable edge-bound design — dreamy on the outside, flexible on the inside.
Watercolour splashes foil-stamped with stars, planets and music notes — controlled chaos with a shimmer finish.
Aged apothecary-style labels in a peel-and-stick roll — organise your life like a Victorian botanist.
Soft florals, pastel covers and gold-foil shimmer on a spiral notebook — meeting notes that look like poetry.
Miffy embroidered beside cherries on a compact zip pouch — two designs, both dangerously giftable.
Botanical stamps plus a full alphabet and numbers — press flowers, butterflies and headlines straight onto the page.
Die-cut memo sheets shaped like your favourite Sanrio friends — leave notes too cute to throw away.
Correction tape wearing pastel Miffy illustrations — typos vanish, cuteness remains.
Embroidered Miffy among strawberries, with a berry-charm zipper — a makeup bag sweet enough to eat (please don't).
Miffy and a scatter of strawberries on a mini pouch — tiny, roomy where it counts, and completely charming.
Miffy nestled in juicy strawberries on your choice of ivory or red — the pencil case equivalent of a summer picnic.
A clear pencil case doodled with Miffy and flowers — see everything you own, all framed in bunny charm.
Pooh with Piglet or Mickey with Minnie inside glossy heart pins — iconic duos, wearable and swoon-ready.
Pentel's silky EnerGel wrapped in four glossy Pikachu designs — limited Candy Quest edition, catch it while you can.
A mystery bag hiding one of eight Pokemon puzzle erasers — take Pikachu apart, rebuild him, erase nothing.
Pikachu, Bulbasaur, Clefairy or Piplup topping a pastel gel pen — pick your starter for the stationery league.
A pop-art hardcover with a magnetic clasp from the Candy Quest drop — Pokemon notes with gallery-wall energy.
Glossy gel pens covered in tiny Pokemon illustrations — smooth ink, maximum pocket-monster morale.
An undated planner scattered with gold-foil stars and moons — start whenever the cosmos (or Monday) allows.
Midnight-black covers, foil constellations, no printed dates — a planner that lets you begin your era anytime.
A soft, absorbent hand towel shaped like Snoopy himself — the happiest thing hanging in your kitchen or bathroom.
